questions.q: Which demon does the goddess Durga slay in the episode celebrated as her central victory?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya recounts how Durga slew the buffalo-demon Mahishasura, earning the name Mahishasuramardini.

questions.q: What form of creature is the demon Mahishasura, slain by Durga, associated with?
questions.explanation: Mahishasura is the shape-shifting buffalo-demon (mahisha means buffalo) defeated by Durga.

questions.q: Which animal serves as the mount (vahana) of the goddess Durga as she battles Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: Durga rides a lion (sometimes depicted as a tiger) into battle against the demon.

questions.q: With which weapon does Durga ultimately pierce the demon Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: Durga pierces Mahishasura with her trident (trishula) to bring about his end.

questions.q: In which sacred text is the story of Durga slaying Mahishasura principally recounted?
questions.explanation: The episode is principally narrated in the Devi Mahatmya, a section of the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: What title does Durga earn for slaying the buffalo-demon Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: For destroying Mahishasura she is honoured as Mahishasuramardini, 'the slayer of Mahishasura'.

questions.q: From whose combined energies is the warrior goddess Durga said to have been formed to slay Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya describes Durga arising from the combined radiance of the devas to vanquish the demon.

questions.q: During which festival is Durga's victory over Mahishasura especially celebrated?
questions.explanation: Durga's triumph over Mahishasura is celebrated during the nine nights of Navaratri.

questions.q: Durga is described as a fierce protective form of which divine figure?
questions.explanation: Durga is the fierce protective form of the Great Goddess, the Devi who embodies Shakti.

questions.q: A notable trait of the demon Mahishasura in his battle with Durga is his ability to do what?
questions.explanation: Mahishasura is a shape-shifting demon, repeatedly changing form during his battle with Durga.

questions.q: What was the essential flaw in the boon that left the demon Mahishasura vulnerable to Durga?
options: It protected him only by day
It protected him only on land
It protected him only from weapons
It protected him only against male foes
questions.explanation: Mahishasura's boon granted near-invincibility against male foes, so a goddess could overcome him.

questions.q: How did Durga come to bear weapons from many different deities in her fight against Mahishasura?
options: Each god bestowed a weapon upon her
She seized them from the demons
They arose from the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya relates that each god gave Durga a weapon, so her many arms bear the gods' arms.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya, which contains the tale of Durga and Mahishasura, forms part of which Purana?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is embedded within the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: Before turning to the goddess, the devas suffered which calamity at the hands of Mahishasura?
options: He conquered the heavens and the three worlds
questions.explanation: Mahishasura overran the heavens and terrorized the three worlds, driving the gods to act.

questions.q: Which of these weapons is traditionally said to have been given to Durga by Shiva for her battles?
questions.explanation: Texts relate that Durga received the trident (trishula) from Shiva, with which she slays Mahishasura.

questions.q: As the goddess Durga closed in, the demon Mahishasura tried to escape her by repeatedly doing what?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya relates that Mahishasura shifted between buffalo, lion, man, and elephant forms to evade Durga before she slew him in his buffalo shape.

questions.q: Which discus, a weapon of Vishnu, is among the arms Durga is depicted wielding against Mahishasura?
questions.explanation: Vishnu is said to have given Durga his Sudarshana Chakra, which appears among her many weapons.

questions.q: The thunderbolt that Durga is sometimes shown wielding against Mahishasura is the weapon of which deity?
questions.explanation: The thunderbolt (Vajra) is the weapon of Indra, king of the devas, given to the goddess for battle.

questions.q: What does the multi-armed iconography of Durga in the Mahishasura battle chiefly express?
options: The passage of the four ages
The combined power of all the gods
questions.explanation: Her many arms, each bearing a god's weapon, express the concentrated power of all the devas in one form.

questions.q: Even as she battles the fierce Mahishasura, the Devi Mahatmya describes Durga's countenance as remaining what?
questions.explanation: The texts describe Durga as serene yet unconquerable, calm even amid the ferocity of battle.

questions.q: Durga belongs to which broad tradition of Hindu worship that venerates the Goddess as supreme?
questions.explanation: Durga is central to Shaktism, the tradition that venerates Shakti, the Great Goddess, as the supreme reality.

questions.q: In the later battles of the Devi Mahatmya, which demon regenerated a clone from every drop of his spilled blood until the goddess Kali drank it?
questions.explanation: In the Devi Mahatmya's account of the war on Shumbha and Nishumbha, Raktabija spawned a clone from each fallen drop of his blood until Kali drank it.

questions.q: From whose brow did the fierce goddess Kali emerge during the wider battle of the Devi Mahatmya?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya relates that Kali emerged from Durga's brow in the battle against the demons.

questions.q: The sage to whom the Markandeya Purana, containing the Devi Mahatmya, is traditionally attributed is which seer?
questions.explanation: The Markandeya Purana, which frames the Devi Mahatmya, is traditionally attributed to the sage Markandeya.

questions.q: The Devi Mahatmya is also widely known by which alternative title?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is also called the Durga Saptashati, named for its roughly seven hundred verses.

questions.q: Durga's name is traditionally connected to her early defeat of a demon bearing which name?
questions.explanation: One tradition links the name Durga to her victory over the demon Durgama; she is the invincible protectress.

questions.q: Within the Devi Mahatmya, the story of Durga and Mahishasura is set out across how many principal episodes or charitas?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya is structured in three charitas (episodes), with the Mahishasura tale in the central one.

questions.q: In the tradition, the radiance of the gods that formed Durga is described as concentrating where before she took shape?
options: Into a single mountain of light
Within a lotus on the ocean
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya describes the gods' combined splendour gathering as a mountain of light from which Durga arose.

questions.q: Beyond the trident, which other weapon, the discus given by Vishnu, is named in the Devi Mahatmya among Durga's arms?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya lists Vishnu's chakra (discus) among the weapons borne in Durga's many hands.

questions.q: Which mountain-dwelling lord is named in tradition as having given Durga the lion that serves as her mount?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Himavan, lord of the Himalayas, gave the goddess the lion she rides into battle.
